name: Davante Adams
headline: Touchdown-dependent vet whose volume and efficiency are quietly fading
body:
Adams turned 14 scores into a WR27 finish over 14 games last year, but the underlying line screams touchdown dependence. His 52.6% catch rate and 1.95 yards after the catch per reception were career worst, and a 0.481 WOPR on a 19.6% target share marked a sharp drop from the 0.541 and 0.804 marks of his prior two seasons. The EPA per play (0.237) and 51.8% success rate look healthy, but they're propped up by 32 red-zone and 23 end-zone looks that funneled into elite scoring rather than steady yardage.

The 14-TD spike is the obvious regression flag; when he didn't find the end zone, his weekly output cratered. In Sean McVay's offense with Matthew Stafford, he still profiles as a clear starter, but Puka Nacua commands the alpha share, and Adams is now 33 with a lingering lower-body issue. Treat him as a touchdown-reliant WR3 in the fifth round, not a foundational piece.
